reviews.review_description: I've been waiting since the game came out on q2 and 3 but man I was thinking that the first major update would be mods and sand box but guess what I've been waiting for so long and I've had no avail. I was so hyped for this game but I am a little irritated that they STILL haven't released the mods or at least the sandbox for Gods sake.

This just isn’t finished. And yeah, that’s not new for game releases but bruh. I think the initial reviews were paid or something because they fooled me. The combat doesn’t feel very satisfying. A lot of failed contact for blows that should’ve been fatal and grabbing opponents is not really possible at the moment. I think it’s one saving grace right now is the combat on horseback. I’m sure this will get better as time goes on and maybe even be good once the mods are available..

So yeah, as the title suggests,worst of all is I can’t get a refund for this. Even when I had only played for 23 minutes.

Update: they have ceased updating. I think they ran with the money? Idk, but this game is dead.

“Hellsplit: Arena” is one of the most brutal, immersive, and adrenaline-pumping VR games ever made. The second you step into its dark medieval world, it feels less like a game and more like surviving inside a nightmare gladiator movie. Every sword swing has weight, every enemy feels dangerous, and the physics-based combat makes each fight insanely satisfying.

What makes Hellsplit: Arena stand out from other VR combat games is how real everything feels. The weapons aren’t floaty toys — axes feel heavy, swords need proper angles to cut effectively, and blocking attacks actually takes timing and skill. The game rewards precision instead of random swinging, which makes victories feel earned. The realistic movement system and detailed body physics make combat unbelievably immersive.  ￼

The atmosphere is another massive reason this game hits so hard. The arenas are dark, creepy, and filled with undead enemies that look genuinely intimidating in VR. The sound design, armor clashing, distant screams, and brutal impact noises make every battle intense. Combined with the graphics, it creates one of the most cinematic VR experiences available. Many VR players even call it one of the best-looking VR games ever made.  ￼

The progression system also keeps the game addictive. You start weak with basic weapons and slowly unlock stronger gear, armor, and brutal new ways to destroy enemies. Unlike sandbox-only VR games, Hellsplit actually gives you a sense of progression and challenge. Mods also expand the experience heavily, adding extra weapons, arenas, and crazy content made by the community.  ￼

What really makes the game unforgettable though is the feeling of power mixed with fear. One second you’re confidently cutting through enemies like a legendary warrior, and the next you’re panicking while surrounded by undead soldiers coming from every direction. Few VR games manage to create that level of tension and immersion.

The game isn’t perfect — some players think combat can feel less responsive compared to games like Blade & Sorcery, and a few people dislike parts of the announcer system or certain enemy behaviors.  ￼ But even with those criticisms, Hellsplit: Arena still delivers one of the most intense melee combat experiences in VR.

Overall, Hellsplit: Arena is a masterpiece for anyone who loves VR combat, dark fantasy worlds, physics-based gameplay, and pure chaos. It feels brutal, cinematic, and insanely immersive in ways most VR games can only dream of achieving. If you own a VR headset and want a game that genuinely makes you feel like a medieval warrior fighting for survival, this is absolutely one of the best choices out there.

Rating: 9/10

You can check out the official Steam page here:  Hellsplit: Arena on Steam￼

This game is honestly a very good game at first. But as you get into it, it slowly gets more boring and boring and boring. The blood is good, the gore is good, and in my opinion, it's too realistic. I would like the swords and weapons for the smaller swords and weapons to have less weight. That way, when you're carrying them, they're not so heavy. The smallest sword in the game is literally has too much weight for only one hand. But overall, the game is really good. It does get very boring after a while of playing. The campaign is very long, but it does get very boring after a while of playing. They still haven't added mods. The game would be better with mods, but This game does get very boring as you play and as you go on. It's just, there's nothing to do and it's just fighting over and over again. It's just the same thing, just fighting and fighting, and that's it. It's just different levels and just, there's nothing really to stay hooked on. It's a good game, but it could, it does need some work.

reviews.review_title: What did they do to you??
reviews.review_description:
When I got this game on pc vr years ago it was incredible, the physics based combat was satisfying, enemies had coherent move sets and parrying was satisfying. The game was honestly many steps above blade and sorcery so when I saw it was being ported to quest after losing access to pc vr I was so excited especially with all the new additions like bows and guns etc.

I was met with immediate dissapointment upon playing, the physics on two handed weapons was butchered, the enemy animations had less polish and were less engaging to fight, I could cheese the entire game with just the pistol now and so much more.

The combat was core to my enjoyment of this game and the decrease in quality had me questioning whether I was looking through my memories of this game with rose tinted glassesbut no. I checked YouTube videos on pc vr hellsplit and it was simply better in every way. Now I understand that there are of course limitations when porting a game to standalone but that does excuse the dip in enjoyment, while I love the guns I’d much rather sacrifice all my fancy new gimmick weapons if it meant better combat 

Sidebote and while many of you won’t care  the game does use a lotion ai imagery  in places that it really wasn’t necessary but it’s not a major thing in gameplay

Depressingly I wouldn’t recommend, at least until the devs improve the port to better replicate the pc version

reviews.review_title: Worst game ever, probably a scam...
reviews.review_description:
I dont understand how this game even made it to market. Its an unfinished joke with horrible graphics and physics; a true scam of a game. Don't buy into the hype, there is nothing redeemable about this game at all. No campaign, no progression, no experience, nothing. You can buy new weapons but none of them feel good in hand nor move like they should.
I seriously think this game is a scam, how can anybody stand behind this and say it's good unless they've been given stacks of cash. 
Grimlord, Skydance Behemoth, Asgard's Wrath, and Arkan Age are all a million times better; dont waste your time and money with this one.

reviews.review_description:
I'm a huge fan of HellSplint Arena, it's honestly one of my favorite ways to pass the time. However, lately, I've been running into some super frustrating issues. Out of nowhere, my character will just die in the campaign (even on old saves) like I'm getting ghosted by invisible enemies. Or I just get stabbed like 10 times all at once by absolute nothing. It's getting ridiculously tiring having to restart from the beginning every time.

I know bugs are a thing, but this is getting real. I hope the devs can jump on this and fix it pronto, I'd hate to see this awesome game go downhill.
I’m lease add sandbox and mods it would be a lot of more fun to be able to do your own scenarios and different maps so please take action￼
